best_edges.action: protect and use as baseline comparator
best_edges.edge: VALID_0DTE pullback_long remains the primary durable edge.
best_edges.evidence: 31 trades, 2404.6 PnL, 251.35 PnL/exposure hour, 12.0 minute median hold, no slow trades. It is the top profile by PnL and second-best meaningful profile by efficiency after a 1-trade call_wall sample.

best_edges.action: protect but split tail behavior before expanding
best_edges.edge: charm_pin_pullback_long is a real fast-turnover edge with a hidden tail pocket.
best_edges.evidence: 34 trades, 1237.7 PnL, 164.67 PnL/exposure hour, 5.3 minute median hold, but worst MAE -527.0. The worst trade had extreme VWAP extension, weak RSI/ADX, and took 80.3 minutes to touch TP.

best_edges.action: protect as small-sample upside breakout edge
best_edges.edge: charm_wall_breakaway_long has strong payoff per trade but insufficient breadth.
best_edges.evidence: 5 trades, 985.6 PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, median TP 12.0. Worst MAE is still heavy at -346.8, so expansion should be paired with structure/timing validation.

best_edges.edge: breakout_long_1dte is a productive 1DTE-only regime rather than fallback noise.
best_edges.evidence: 20 trades, 1298.7 PnL, 167.94 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow trades. Hidden MAE signatures show removing this profile retains only 81.1% of PnL, so it should not be pruned casually.

best_edges.edge: squeeze_long appears clean enough to preserve while testing expansion cautiously.
best_edges.evidence: 10 trades, 721.0 PnL, 187.16 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -208.25, median setup quality 69.16. It is less tail-heavy than pullback/charm profiles, but sample size is modest.

broken_edges.action: do not broaden until stale/no-0DTE routing is separately validated
broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE produced only 145.8 PnL across 5 trades and 53.6 to 90.1 PnL/exposure hour in no-0DTE profiles, materially below VALID_0DTE and VALID_1DTE_ONLY.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback is profitable but low-efficiency and under-sampled.

broken_edges.action: freeze unless explicitly used for dormant-regime activation tests
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long all produced zero trades in this run while active-listed or configured.
broken_edges.problem: Multiple router-listed profiles are dead or unreachable under current gates, adding search space without evidence.
broken_edges.profile: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, trend_day_pullback_long

broken_edges.action: do not treat raw win rate as proof of safety
broken_edges.evidence: All completed trades won, but average MAE is -99.48, worst MAE is -527.0, only 9.4% had MFE before MAE, and only 0.9% touched TP before MAE.
broken_edges.problem: The no-stop-loss structure hides large adverse excursion behind eventual profitable exits.

broken_edges.action: avoid broad ADX-slope or setup floors as global filters
broken_edges.evidence: ADX slope >= 0 would block 42 trades, 2815.8 PnL, and 24 protected good trades while only improving worst MAE to -375.7. Setup score >= 62 would retain only 43.0% PnL in the worst-tail check.
broken_edges.problem: Single-threshold risk pruning destroys too much edge relative to tail improvement.

market_regime_read: The current leader is a long-only, positive-expectancy momentum/pullback complex in a favorable sample: 106 trades, 6884.4 PnL, 35.5 exposure hours, no slow >=12h trades, and 100% realized win rate. The real regime split is data-state and profile-specific, not a simple global indicator threshold. VALID_0DTE pullbacks drive most profit, VALID_1DTE_ONLY breakout is independently useful, and STALE_0DTE fallback is low-return insurance. Tail risk clusters around entries that are extended from VWAP, slow to profit, or have poor early path behavior, but many clean winners also share superficially similar extension/structure readings. The next research should discover profile splits and dormant concepts, not globally tighten the system.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate charm-pin pullbacks into fast-clean pullbacks versus extended delayed-recovery pin trades.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May overfit the single -527 MAE event if implemented as a hard threshold without enough out-of-sample bad-tail coverage.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Fast Recovery Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Profile, ES VWAP distance, pullback-from-high, RSI slope, ADX, early MFE/MAE path diagnostics where available.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long entry with extended VWAP distance or weak RSI/ADX but otherwise valid charm-pin setup.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE and hold-time tail while preserving at least 85-90% of its PnL and keeping median hold near current 5.3 minutes.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Test dormant pullback_long_1dte as a 1DTE complement to breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Could cannibalize the existing 1DTE breakout edge or add low-quality overnight/session trades if router priority overlaps poorly.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries, 1DTE option map fields, setup quality, ES structure, hold time, MAE by 1DTE profile.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_1DTE_ONLY data state when breakout_long_1dte is not selected or when pullback structure is cleaner than breakout structure.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Incremental 1DTE PnL/exposure hour above 150 with no degradation of breakout_long_1dte PnL and worst MAE no worse than -375.7.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Activate mean_reversion_long only as an inactive dormant-profile research probe for late pullback/reclaim regimes.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Mean reversion may conflict with the existing long momentum router and add trades in weak trend states that the current system correctly avoids.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Mean Reversion Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE entries blocked by pullback/reclaim logic, ES distance from VWAP, rolling-high distance, RSI bucket, ADX bucket, realized MAE/MFE.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE state with extended pullback from high and non-catastrophic VWAP distance where existing active profiles are idle.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 10 trades in replay/backtest, positive PnL/exposure hour above 150, average MAE below current -99.5, and no new worst-MAE record.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Investigate whether a path-aware tighten/exit logic can reduce no-stop tail without blocking entries.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Requires code and may conflict with no-stop-loss premise if it effectively becomes a hidden stop or premature loss realization.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Delayed Profit Failure Tightener
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path MFE/MAE samples, first +1.5 point time, half-TP touch time, first 15m MFE/MAE, profile and data state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Entry has not reached small positive excursion within expected profile-specific window and early MAE expands before MFE.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Lower average MAE and exposure hours with no more than 5% reduction in net PnL; specifically reduce 60-180 minute hold bucket and tail MAE.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Explore call_wall_acceptance_long expansion as a high-quality acceptance regime.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Current evidence is only 1 trade, so expansion may expose a noisy or rare condition rather than a true edge.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Expansion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Call wall distance, acceptance seconds, option call-volume increase, forward DEX ratio, setup quality, ES VWAP distance.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE with clean call-wall acceptance and strong option participation, especially where current profile routes are idle.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Generate at least 5-10 trades while maintaining PnL/exposure hour above 250 and worst MAE materially below pullback/charm tails.

next_experiments.config_bias: Config-only activation of dormant pullback_long_1dte through the 1DTE router, with conservative profile gates and no changes to VALID_0DTE profile behavior.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether 1DTE pullbacks are a distinct profitable data-state edge beside breakout_long_1dte.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The 20-trade 1DTE breakout edge implies the 1DTE-only data state is viable; a pullback-specific 1DTE profile may capture trades the breakout profile misses without increasing 0DTE tail risk.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Dormant Activation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Incremental robust score and 1DTE PnL/exposure hour, with breakout_long_1dte PnL unchanged or improved.
next_experiments.risk: Router overlap may displace proven breakout_long_1dte trades or add slow low-efficiency fallback behavior.

next_experiments.config_bias: Profile-specific split/tightening around charm_pin_pullback_long only; avoid global ADX/setup floors. Prefer a narrow extended-VWAP rejection or charm-pin tail-risk condition that targets the -527 MAE fingerprint.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long tail MAE while preserving its fast 5.3 minute median-hold edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The charm-pin tail comes from a separable extended/weak-momentum pocket, not from the core charm-pin pullback edge.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Tail Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE and average MAE, with retained profile PnL at least 85%.
next_experiments.risk: The same profile also contains clean extended-VWAP winners, so blunt VWAP-distance pruning can remove too much edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Config-only activation probe for call_wall_acceptance_long expansion or adjacent dormant wall/acceptance routing, keeping trade count small and gates strict.
next_experiments.goal: Test whether the one high-efficiency call-wall sample is expandable or just noise.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Call wall acceptance may be a cleaner momentum acceptance regime with lower MAE than pullback/charm profiles when option participation confirms.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Acceptance Breadth Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Trade count, PnL/exposure hour, and worst MAE for call_wall_acceptance_long.
next_experiments.risk: One-trade evidence is fragile; expansion should be rejected quickly if MAE rises toward current profile tails.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep stale/no-0DTE routing active but test stricter fallback-only quality or routing split; do not alter VALID_0DTE profile thresholds in the same experiment.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether STALE_0DTE fallback should remain broad, shrink, or be isolated to the cleanest fallback profile.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE contributes low-efficiency PnL and longer holds; a stricter fallback router may improve risk-adjusted score without hurting the main edge.
next_experiments.name: No-0DTE Fallback Isolation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure hour and average MAE, while total net profit remains at least 98% of base.
next_experiments.risk: Only 5 stale trades exist, so the result may be sample noise and should not dominate router design.

next_experiments.config_bias: Small activation test for inactive VALID_0DTE dormant concept mean_reversion_long or currently dead wall_reclaim_long, but only one dormant/dead concept per run.
next_experiments.goal: Find a new VALID_0DTE regime when existing profiles are idle rather than over-tuning the current winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some blocked/idle VALID_0DTE states may support a lower-MAE reclaim or mean-reversion edge that is not represented by current active trade distribution.
next_experiments.name: Dormant VALID_0DTE Regime Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: New-profile PnL/exposure hour, trade count, and worst MAE versus current VALID_0DTE baseline.
next_experiments.risk: Dead-profile activation can pollute the router with low-quality trades and obscure the proven pullback/charm/squeeze edges.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not activate a global threshold; instead run a diagnostic config that logs or isolates extended-vwap-rejection near misses by profile if available through existing logging flags.
next_experiments.goal: Validate whether extended-VWAP rejection threshold misses are a reusable bad-MAE signature or just post-hoc clustering.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The enabled extended-VWAP rejection veto almost matched all six worst MAE entries; small profile-aware boundary changes may reduce tail risk better than global gates.
next_experiments.name: Extended VWAP Rejection Near-Miss Validation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Number of bad-MAE trades blocked per good protected trade lost, plus retained PnL percentage.
next_experiments.risk: Several protected winners are also extended above VWAP, so overbroad boundary expansion can sacrifice the system's fastest profits.

research_notes: The best next work is not raw profit expansion. The leaderboard is stable and already high-scoring, but all top candidates share the same unresolved -527 MAE. Since no single global threshold cleanly improves the risk-adjusted profile, the plan should test regime discovery: 1DTE companion profile, charm-pin split, no-0DTE fallback isolation, and one dormant VALID_0DTE concept at a time. Treat all 100% win-rate evidence as conditional on the no-stop exit model; require MAE, exposure hours, and early path behavior in every acceptance criterion.
risk_controls: Never combine dormant activation with broad global threshold changes in the same experiment; attribution would be unreadable.
Protect pullback_long baseline behavior unless an experiment explicitly targets it; it is the largest durable edge.
Reject experiments that improve worst MAE only by sacrificing more than roughly 15-20% of total PnL without a compensating exposure-hour reduction.
For charm_pin_pullback_long, prefer profile-local split or veto validation over global setup quality, ADX slope, or VWAP-distance floors.
Require separate reporting by option data state: VALID_0DTE, VALID_1DTE_ONLY, and STALE_0DTE.
Treat call_wall_acceptance_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long as small-sample edges until they produce materially more trades.
Do not optimize inactive profiles absent from the router lists; dormant activation should be limited to explicitly listed dormant concepts from the activation context.
